title: Vijaydurg Fort
tagline: Vijaydurg Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Vijaydurg (sometimes written as Viziadurg), the oldest fort on the Sindhudurg coast, was constructed during the regime of Raja Bhoja II of the Shilahar dynasty (construction period
overview.description: Vijaydurg (sometimes written as Viziadurg), the oldest fort on the Sindhudurg coast, was constructed during the regime of Raja Bhoja II of the Shilahar dynasty (construction period 1193-1205) and restructured by Shivaji. Earlier, the fort encompassed an area of 5 acres (20,000 m2) and was surrounded by sea on all four sides. Over the years the eastern trench was reclaimed and a road constructed thereon. Presently the area of fort is about 17 acres (69,000 m2) and is surrounded by the Arabian Sea on three sides.

topPlaces.name: Rameshwar Wadi
topPlaces.description: Rameshwar also known as Rameshwar Wadi is a small town located on the coast of Sindhudurg District of Maharashtra on the west coast of India. A very old Shri Dev Rameshwar Temple is located in this town which is dedicated to the Hindu deity Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Vijaydurg
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Vijaydurg was fought between Tulaji Angre, the Admiral of the Maratha Navy and the combined forces of the British East India Company and the Maratha Empire led by Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao in early 1756.

topPlaces.name: Vijaydurg (town)
topPlaces.description: Vijaydurg (formerly also known as Gheriah) is a seaport 485 km from Mumbai on India's West Coast. It boasts a safe harbour with average depths in excess of 100 feet.

topPlaces.name: Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project
topPlaces.description: Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project is a proposed nuclear power plant in India. If built, it would be the largest nuclear power generating station in the world by net generation capacity, at 9,900 MW. As of 2025, each unit's power has been increased to 1730MW and the installed capacity has been raised to 10,380MW. The power project is proposed by Nuclear Power Corporation of India (NPCIL) and would be built at Madban village of Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Katradevi
topPlaces.description: Katradevi is a village in Rajapur taluka in Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Ambolgad
topPlaces.description: Ambolgad is a village near Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project in the Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra, India. Ambolgad has very scenic beach in Ratnagiri near Nate.

items.note: Rameshwar also known as Rameshwar Wadi is a small town located on the coast of Sindhudurg District of Maharashtra on the west coast of India. A very old Shri Dev Rameshwar Temple is located in this town which is dedicated to the Hindu deity Shiva.

The Battle of Vijaydurg was fought between Tulaji Angre, the Admiral of the Maratha Navy and the combined forces of the British East India Company and the Maratha Empire led by Peshwa Balaji Baji Rao in early 1756.
Vijaydurg (formerly also known as Gheriah) is a seaport 485 km from Mumbai on India's West Coast. It boasts a safe harbour with average depths in excess of 100 feet.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vijaydurg Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vijaydurg Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vijaydurg Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kolhapur Airport (103 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (139 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vijaydurg Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vijaydurg Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vijaydurg Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Vijaydurg Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vijaydurg Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Rameshwar Wadi, Battle of Vijaydurg, Vijaydurg (town), Jaitapur Nuclear Power Project, Katradevi.
